description: Add background mode and stop command to the OpenTabs CLI. Currently `opentabs start` runs in the foreground only, blocking the terminal. Users working with MCP clients (also terminal processes) need two terminal tabs. This adds `opentabs start --background` to detach the server and `opentabs stop` to shut it down. A PID file at ~/.opentabs/server.pid tracks the background process.

userStories.title: Add PID file helper to shared constants and CLI config
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I want a shared helper for the PID file path so that both start and stop commands use the same location.
acceptanceCriteria: A `getPidFilePath` function is exported from @opentabs-dev/shared that returns `~/.opentabs/server.pid` (or `$OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR/server.pid`)
The function follows the same pattern as `getLogFilePath` and `getConfigPath`
The function is re-exported from platform/cli/src/config.ts alongside the other path helpers
userStories.notes:
File: platform/shared/src/constants.ts (line 46 area). Add a new function after `getLogFilePath`:

```ts
export const getPidFilePath = (): string => join(getConfigDir(), 'server.pid');
```

Export it from platform/shared/src/index.ts (line 30 area, alongside getConfigDir, getLogFilePath, etc.).

Re-export from platform/cli/src/config.ts (line 18) â€” add `getPidFilePath` to the re-export list from @opentabs-dev/shared.

userStories.title: Add --background flag to opentabs start
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `opentabs start --background` so that the server detaches to the background and I get my terminal back.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs start --background` starts the MCP server as a detached child process
The detached process's PID is written to ~/.opentabs/server.pid
stdout/stderr of the detached process are redirected to ~/.opentabs/server.log (not the terminal)
The CLI prints a confirmation message like 'Server started in background (PID: 12345)' and exits with code 0
The first-time setup instructions still print before the server detaches (so the user sees MCP configs)
If the server is already running (port in use), the --background flag still shows the port-conflict error
The PID file is cleaned up when the server exits (via process exit handler in the spawned process)
Running `opentabs start` (without --background) continues to work exactly as before (foreground mode, no PID file)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts.

The key change is in `handleStart`. When `options.background` is true:
1. Still do all the same initialization (autoInitialize, ensureAuthSecret, isPortInUse check)
2. Still print the startup banner and first-time instructions (so user sees MCP configs)
3. Instead of spawning with `teeStream` to the terminal, spawn detached:
   ```ts
   const logStream = createWriteStream(logFilePath, { flags: 'a' });
   const child = spawn(platformExec('node'), [serverEntry], {
     env,
     stdio: ['ignore', logStream, logStream],
     detached: true,
   });
   child.unref();
   ```
4. Write the PID to `getPidFilePath()`:
   ```ts
   await writeFile(getPidFilePath(), String(child.pid));
   ```
5. Print confirmation and exit:
   ```ts
   console.log(`Server started in background (PID: ${child.pid})`);
   console.log(pc.dim(`Logs: ${logFilePath}`));
   console.log(pc.dim(`Stop: opentabs stop`));
   ```

Add the option to `registerStartCommand`:
```ts
.option('--background', 'Start the server in the background')
```

Add `background?: boolean` to the `StartOptions` interface.

For PID file cleanup: the spawned server process should remove the PID file on exit. One approach is to use a wrapper that writes the PID file and cleans up, or to add a small SIGTERM/exit handler. Since the server entry point is `@opentabs-dev/mcp-server`, the simplest approach is to have the start command write the PID file after spawn and have the stop command clean it up after killing.

Import `writeFile` from `node:fs/promises` and `getPidFilePath` from `../config.js`.

userStories.title: Add opentabs stop command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `opentabs stop` to shut down the MCP server so that I don't have to find and kill the process manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs stop` when a server is running (with PID file) sends SIGTERM to the process and waits for exit
After the process exits, the PID file is removed
A success message is printed: 'Server stopped.'

Running `opentabs stop` when no PID file exists falls back to checking the port: sends a request to the health endpoint, and if a server is found, prints 'Server is running but was not started with --background. Stop it with Ctrl+C in the terminal where it is running.'
Running `opentabs stop` when the PID file exists but the process is not running (stale PID) cleans up the PID file and prints 'Server is not running (stale PID file cleaned up).'
The --port option is respected for the fallback health check

userStories.title: Update status and doctor to show PID info for background servers
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs status` and `opentabs doctor` to indicate when the server is running in background mode so I know how to manage it.
acceptanceCriteria: When a PID file exists and the server is running, `opentabs status` shows a 'PID' line in the output (e.g., 'PID             12345 (background)')
When a PID file exists and the server is running, `opentabs doctor` shows a passing check for the server that includes '(background, PID 12345)'
When a PID file exists but is stale (process not running), `opentabs status` does NOT show PID info (shows normal 'Server not running' message) and the stale PID file is cleaned up
When no PID file exists (foreground mode), no PID line is shown â€” existing behavior unchanged
